title: Student software team
subtitle: Master course peer review tool, lightweight Scrum
source_label: Own facilitation notes from the project
operational_note: Sprint and impediment rows are stored in this repository. Numbers are consistent with the KPI cards (13/21 then 16/16 points).

artifacts.label: Full case study (how I facilitated the team)
Working agreement used by the team
Raw sprint json in this project

evidence.claim: Sprint throughput improved from 62% to 100% of planned points
Planning time capped at 90 minutes after retro action
evidence.excerpt: Retro sprint 1 action: Cap planning at 90 minutes and use a Definition of Done checklist.

evidence.claim: Facilitation role separate from main development
evidence.excerpt: Author focused on team process; implementation continued in the peer review repo.

context: Five master students built a peer review tool. Code was mostly fine but coordination was messy. I focused on how the team worked, similar to a junior Scrum Master role.
before_summary: Long planning, silent blockers in chat, tests skipped near deadlines.
after_summary: Capped planning, daily standup, impediment log, one retro action per sprint.
changes: Retro with exactly one action item

sprints.goal: Ship peer review module with basic tests
sprints.notes: Planning too long, team tired before coding started

sprints.goal: Green CI and smaller backlog items
sprints.notes: Retro action on planning time limit actually helped

sprints.goal: Demo ready build for course milestone
sprints.notes: First sprint where review did not overrun

impediments.title: CI pipeline red for two days, nobody owned the fix
impediments.notes: came up in daily, we assigned one person

impediments.title: Acceptance criteria missing on three backlog items
impediments.notes: planning ran long because stories were vague

retros.action: Cap planning at 90 minutes and use a Definition of Done checklist
sad: We merged without running tests locally

retros.action: Split stories before planning if estimate is above 5 points
glad: Impediment log helped spot CI issue early
sad: One story was too big and rolled over
mad: Slack replaced the daily some days

demo_note: The peer review app is not on a public URL yet. Clone the delivery repo and run npm run dev (localhost:3000), or deploy it to Vercel.

timeline.title: Before: unclear ownership
timeline.detail: Planning ran past 2 hours. Blockers lived in Slack threads.

timeline.title: Working agreement introduced
timeline.detail: Daily at fixed time, DoD written down, impediment log started.

timeline.title: Sprint 1 retro action shipped
timeline.detail: Planning capped at 90 minutes. First sprint logged as 13/21 points.

timeline.title: Delivery stabilises
timeline.detail: CI blocker closed in daily. Sprint 2 finishes 17/18 points.

timeline.title: After: demo ready
timeline.detail: Sprint 3 at 16/16 points. Zero open impediments at sprint end.

stakeholder_quotes.quote: Once every blocker had a name next to it, we stopped talking in circles in chat.
Stories started arriving with acceptance criteria before planning, not during the demo week.
I was not the main feature developer. I kept the team rhythm and made problems visible early.

github_activity.note: Live delivery repo for the peer review app. Commits below are public on GitHub.

commits.message: Fix admin login redirect loop (signed tokens for serverless)
Fix database schema: security question migration on registration
Add automatic database migration on registration and lookup

visual_artifacts.note: Rendered from team notes in this repo (not stock photos). Replace with your own screenshots in web/img/evidence/ if you have them.
